honest-observe/src/honest_observe/devtoo: _NS_PER_MS is a division denominator for a millisecond display rounded to one decimal (_ms) or whole ms (_whole_ms); shifting it by 1 part in a million leaves every rounded display string identical. Verified _ms unchanged.
As above: a 1-part-in-a-million change to the ms denominator does not alter any rounded display string. Verified.

The framework timestamps are millisecond-precision ISO (HH:MM:SS.mmm), so after rstrip('Z') the clock portion is exactly 12 characters; [:12] and [:13] return the same string. Verified on a ms timestamp.

Emptying the 'Z' in rstrip('Z') is a no-op here: the trailing Z sits at index 12 of the split clock portion, so the [:12] slice already excludes it. The result is identical. Verified on a ms timestamp.
Removing the trailing `return None` in _request_id_of (reached when an event carries no request_id anywhere) lets it fall off its end and return None implicitly — identical.
honest-observe/src/honest_observe/events: TypedDict total= is a static-only annotation; at runtime total=True and total=False produce an identical class whose instances are plain dicts with no required-key enforcement. build_event assembles the envelope explicitly, so no constructible input distinguishes them.
